title: Star Trek Meets Space Shuttle
story: Beam me up, NASA! In 1976, the space agency unveiled its first space shuttle with a stellar surprise. title: Harriet Tubman's Daring Escape to Freedom
story: Harriet Tubman refused to live in chains. On September 17, 1849, she made a bold decision that would change history. Tubman's courage and determination would soon make her a legendary conductor on the Underground Railroad, helping many others find freedom.

title: Tiny Life Discovered!

title: Herschel's Icy Moon Discovery
story: Gazing through his powerful telescope, astronomer William Herschel spotted something new in the night sky. It was September 17, 1789, and he had just discovered Mimas, a small, icy moon orbiting Saturn. This frozen world, shaped like a giant snowball, became famous for its uncanny resemblance to the Death Star from Star Wars. Herschel's keen eye had unveiled another of our solar system's hidden wonders.

title: Radar Pioneer Sees Beyond the Horizon
story: Oswald Garrison Villard Jr. was born on September 17, 1916, in Dobbs Ferry, New York. This brilliant American engineer would go on to revolutionize radar technology. title: Zond 5's Lunar Adventure
story: A space capsule named Zond 5 zoomed around the Moon and back to Earth on September 17, 1968. This Soviet spacecraft carried a special cargo: turtles, flies, and plants! activity_question: What type of show was 'Mission Impossible'?
bonus_fact: The show's famous theme song is one of the most recognizable TV tunes ever created.

title: Zappa's Final Bow: A Musical Triumph
story: The crowd roared with excitement as Frank Zappa took the stage in Frankfurt, Germany on September 17, 1992. The legendary musician conducted The Ensemble Modern, performing parts of his classical masterpiece 'The Yellow Shark'. As the last notes faded, the audience erupted into a thunderous standing ovation lasting nearly twenty minutes. Little did they know, this electrifying performance would be Zappa's final public appearance.
activity_question: What was the name of Frank Zappa's classical work performed in Frankfurt?
bonus_fact: Frank Zappa was known for his unique blend of rock, jazz, and classical music styles.

title: Mexico's Treasure Trove of History Opens
story: Imagine a place where ancient treasures come to life! In 1964, Mexico City unveiled its crown jewel: the National Museum of Anthropology. On September 17, President Adolfo López Mateos proudly opened the doors to this massive museum, the largest in all of Latin America. Visitors can explore incredible artifacts and learn about Mexico's rich cultural heritage.
activity_question: What is the National Museum of Anthropology known for being?
activity_choices: The largest museum in Latin America
The most visited museum in the world
The museum with the most dinosaur bones
activity_answer: The largest museum in Latin America
follow_up_question: What kinds of artifacts do you think you might see in an anthropology museum?

title: Music Spins Faster: A Symphony Revolution
story: Beethoven's Fifth Symphony got a speedy makeover in 1934. On September 17, RCA Victor unveiled the first 33 1/3 rpm record. This new technology let the Philadelphia Orchestra's powerful performance, led by Leopold Stokowski, spin out in crystal-clear sound. Music lovers could now enjoy longer recordings without flipping discs as often.
activity_question: What famous symphony was featured on this groundbreaking record?
bonus_fact: 33 1/3 rpm records could play for about 30 minutes per side, much longer than older formats.
